response.justification: While this candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and exceeds the minimum experience threshold (19+ years with a Bachelor's degree), there is a critical domain mismatch. The role requires expertise in SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, and hardware acceleration for AI workloads—areas completely absent from the resume. The candidate's background is entirely web, full-stack, and application-focused, with only 6 months of C/C++ systems work (at a measurement software company, not hardware design). Recent AI exposure at re:cinq is software-level MCP frameworks, not hardware accelerator optimization.
strengths: Extensive career experience (19+ years) exceeding minimum qualification with demonstrated software craftsmanship, TDD, and CI/CD expertise
Some performance optimization and low-latency systems background (Dunnhumby microservices, async I/O, caching patterns)
Recent exposure to AI infrastructure and modern development practices relevant to technical breadth
concerns: Zero evidence of SoC, IP modeling, or semiconductor design experience—core technical requirement for the role
No GPU architecture, hardware accelerator, or deep neural network training/inference optimization background
Career trajectory entirely focused on web, mobile, and application-level software; no systems-on-chip or hardware-software co-design work
Recent career gap (10 months caregiving) and no indication of continued involvement in hardware performance topics

key_factors.factor: General software engineering maturity and testing rigor
